text.title: ClickUp Setup Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding ClickUp Setup. Each pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale; HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override.

rule.statement: ≤3 Spaces (e.g. eng / ops / sales). More Spaces = workspace fragmentation.
rule.rationale: Space proliferation kills cross-team views + reports; ≤3 keeps the workspace navigable.

rule.statement: Custom statuses: ≤7 (e.g. backlog / todo / in-progress / blocked / review / done / cancelled). Per-list status sets bankrupt the workspace.
rule.rationale: More statuses = reports break; per-list custom states are the most common 'why-did-we-leave-ClickUp' reason.

rule.statement: ≤10 automations per Space. Each automation is a maintenance tax.
rule.rationale: Automation sprawl is invisible work; cap forces discipline.

rule.statement: Turn on only essentials: Time Tracking, Custom Fields, Dependencies, Sprints. Disable rest.
rule.rationale: ClickUp's default-on ClickApps create UI clutter that slows every interaction.

rule.statement: ≤5 views per List (e.g. Board / List / Calendar / Timeline / Box). More = analysis paralysis.
rule.rationale: View proliferation hides authoritative view; ≤5 keeps team mental model stable.

rule.statement: If preconditions in AGENTS.md "Applies If" do not all hold, OR any "Skip If" disqualifier is true, do not run this methodology — route to the named alternative.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are pre-condition gated; running them out-of-scope produces noise and burns the agent context budget.

rule.statement: If preconditions hold, execute the procedure in `content/04-procedure.xml` (or the rule set above when complexity is light), emit the artefact specified by `content/02-output-contract.xml`, and validate with `scripts/validate-clickup-setup.py` before returning.
rule.rationale: Tying the entry rule to the validator script closes the loop between policy and machine check.
